        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After two RIGHT presses, something weird happened:
- The green-framed box with W center seems to have merged with the green-framed box with B center
- Looking at row 30: `NNWNNN` at cols 24-29
- The two green boxes seem to have overlapped/merged
- The segmentation shows:
  - id=6: N, 5 pixels, boundary [30,24]-[32,25] (left part of merged shape)
  - id=7: W, 4 pixels (white center, now merged)
  - id=8: N, 8 pixels, boundary [30,27]-[32,29] (right part - the B-centered box)
  - id=10: B, 1 pixel at [31,28] (black center of B box)
So the green box with W center moved right by 6 total (from cols 18-20 to cols 24-26), and now it's overlapping with the green box with B center (at cols 27-29).
This is getting complicated. Let me reset and think more carefully.
Let me undo with LEFT twice to get back to original state.
